aliases: Italian sausage with salad and potato wedges

english: Italian sausage with salad and potato wedges

food_subgroup: Processed and cured pork sausage, grilled

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Italian grilled pork sausage (salsiccia alla griglia); closest parent 'pork sausage food product' (FOODON_03302757) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pork sausages) is the closest parent; no specific code for Italian-style grilled salsiccia exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 'Pork sausage (food)' is the closest available term; no specific concept for salsiccia alla griglia exists.

serving.description: 1 plate (sausage with salad, potato wedges, and mushroom sauce)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical restaurant serving of salsiccia alla griglia is approximately 350 g, comprising two to three grilled pork sausage links (roughly 150 g), a side of mixed salad (approximately 80 g), crispy potato wedges (approximately 90 g), and mushroom sauce (approximately 30 g).

nutrient_source.title: Pork, fresh, ground, cooked, pan-broiled (USDA SR Legacy, NDB 10219) and Italian pork sausage, cooked (USDA FoodData Central, FDC ID 167878)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked Italian pork sausage (FDC ID 167878, 'Sausage, Italian, pork, cooked') as the closest approved-source match. Values represent the sausage component per 100 g cooked weight. The side components (salad, potato wedges, mushroom sauce) are not included in the per-100g figures, which reflect the sausage itself as the primary food item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for salsiccia alla griglia. GI of 28 is assigned by analogy to processed pork sausage / frankfurter-type products as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Sausages are predominantly protein and fat with minimal carbohydrate, yielding a low GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (28 × 2.0 g per 100 g × 1.5 sausage portion 150 g / 100) / 100 ≈ 0.84, rounded to 1.6 for the full 350 g plate including potato wedges contribution estimated at approximately 10 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (665 mg per 100 g in the sausage component alone)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid salsiccia alla griglia due to its high sodium content from curing and seasoning; a single restaurant serving may contribute 1,000–1,500 mg sodium, approaching or exceeding the recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index but high saturated fat and caloric density
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the sausage itself has a low glycemic index (GI ~28) and minimal carbohydrate, the high saturated fat content (8.8 g per 100 g) and the accompanying potato wedges increase the overall glycemic and caloric load of the meal, warranting portion control for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (8.8 g/100 g), high sodium (665 mg/100 g), and processed meat classification
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salsiccia alla griglia is a processed red meat product high in saturated fat and sodium; frequent consumption is associated with increased cardiovascular risk, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake in line with WHO and AHA dietary guidelines for processed meats.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salsiccia alla Griglia is a traditional Italian grilled pork sausage dish. 'Salsiccia' refers to fresh Italian pork sausage, typically made from coarsely ground pork and pork fat seasoned with salt, black pepper, fennel seeds, and sometimes garlic or wine. 'Alla griglia' means 'grilled' in Italian. The sausages are marinated and then cooked over a grill or griddle, developing a charred, smoky exterior while remaining juicy inside. In the restaurant context served in Singapore (Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore, Boat Quay), the dish is presented as a full plate comprising grilled salsiccia links, a mixed green salad, crispy potato wedges, and a mushroom sauce on the side. Nutritionally, the sausage component is energy-dense (approximately 304 kcal per 100 g), high in fat (24.8 g/100 g, of which 8.8 g is saturated), and moderate in protein (16.7 g/100 g), with low carbohydrate content (2.0 g/100 g). Sodium is high at 665 mg per 100 g due to curing and seasoning. The glycemic index is low (~28) because the food is predominantly protein and fat. Clinically, this dish is of concern for individuals with hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat content. It is not suitable for halal or kosher diets and contains pork. The dish is representative of Italian trattoria-style grilled meat courses (secondi piatti).
common_user_queries: How many calories in Italian grilled sausage?
What is the sodium content of Italian pork sausage?
Is grilled Italian sausage high in fat?
Can diabetics eat salsiccia alla griglia?
What does salsiccia alla griglia taste like?
What is the GI of Italian sausage?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the closest approved USDA FoodData Central entry for cooked Italian pork sausage (FDC ID 167878), which is a strong match for the sausage component. The full plate includes sides (salad, potato wedges, mushroom sauce) not reflected in the per-100g figures, which represent the sausage only. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for processed pork sausage products. No Singapore-specific or HPB Foodo entry exists for this dish.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values represent the grilled Italian pork sausage component only, sourced from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 167878 ('Sausage, Italian, pork, cooked'). The accompanying mixed salad, potato wedges, and mushroom sauce are not included in the per-100g figures. 2. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 for the sausage component; this is confirmed zero per USDA source as processed pork sausage contains negligible dietary fibre. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this product. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 6. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g per 100 g reflects naturally occurring trans fats from ruminant animal fat present in pork products at trace levels per USDA data. 7. GI of 28 is an analogous estimate based on processed pork sausage data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). 8. The input food_group 'Grains and staples' has been corrected to 'Meat and alternatives' as salsiccia alla griglia is a pork sausage dish. 9. This dish is not halal (contains pork) and not suitable for vegetarian or vegan diets. 10. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Italian cuisine dish; no Singaporean-language mandatory fields apply. The romanized Italian name is preserved.
